file_name: Yang et al. - 2021 - Deep hybrid convolutional neural network for segmentation of melanoma skin lesion.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper used deep learning, specifically a proposed 'EfficientUNet++' model (combining EfficientNet and UNet++ architectures), to perform automatic segmentation of melanoma skin lesions. The method was evaluated on the PH2 and ISIC-2018 datasets, achieving Dice coefficients of 93% and 96% respectively.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is explicitly labeled as a 'Research Article' and presents an original experimental study proposing a new algorithm ('EfficientUNet++') for melanoma segmentation. It includes specific sections for Methods, Experiment (with datasets PH2 and ISIC-2018), Results (Dice coefficients, IoU, loss values), and Discussion, rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Deep Hybrid Convolutional Neural Network for Segmentation of Melanoma Skin Lesion
brief_summary: This study proposes EfficientUNet++, a deep hybrid convolutional neural network for the automatic segmentation of melanoma skin lesions, combining EfficientNet and UNet++ architectures. The model was evaluated on the PH2 and ISIC-2018 datasets to compare its performance against other common segmentation models. On the PH2 dataset, EfficientUNet++ achieved a Dice coefficient of 93% and an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 96%, outperforming comparative models. On the ISIC-2018 dataset, it obtained a Dice coefficient of 96% and an IoU of 94%, demonstrating superior or comparable performance with better IoU and loss values. The results indicate that EfficientUNet++ efficiently detects skin lesions by improving composite coefficients and utilizing residual units to deepen the network.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on the automatic segmentation of melanoma skin lesions. The datasets used (PH2 and ISIC-2018) contain images of skin lesions, with the PH2 dataset specifically noted for having clinical diagnoses and the ISIC-2018 dataset being associated with melanoma detection challenges. The paper treats the segmentation target as melanoma lesions.

all_task_types.details: The paper proposes EfficientUNet++ for the automatic segmentation of melanoma skin lesions. The model predicts pixel-wise segmentation masks for skin lesions. Performance was evaluated using Dice coefficients and Intersection over Union (IoU) on the PH2 and ISIC-2018 datasets. The text explicitly distinguishes image segmentation from image classification or object recognition.

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2018
datasets_used.description: The International Skin Imaging Collaboration 2018 dataset, specifically the 'Skin Lesion Analysis Towards Melanoma Detection' grand challenge subset. It contains expert annotations, skin lesion images, and mask images.

use.validation: Used for validation during training (loss and Dice coefficient curves monitored).
use.testing: Used for testing the model's performance (200 images).

datasets_used.name: PH2 Dataset
datasets_used.description: A dataset of skin images obtained from the Pediatric Department of Pedro Hispano Hospital in Matosinhos, Portugal. It includes manual segmentation masks and clinical diagnoses.

use.training: Used for training the EfficientUNet++ model (160 images).
use.validation: Used for validation during training (ablation tests performed on PH2 data validation set).
use.testing: Used for testing the model's performance (40 images).

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To analyze the performance contribution of specific components (modules and activation functions) by removing or replacing them.
To prevent overfitting during model training by monitoring the loss function and saving weights at the point of optimal performance before overfitting occurs.
To evaluate segmentation performance by calculating True Positives (TP), False Positives (FP), True Negatives (TN), and False Negatives (FN) to derive metrics like accuracy and IoU.
To quantify the similarity between the predicted segmentation mask and the ground truth mask.
To measure the overlap between the predicted segmentation and the ground truth.

statistical_methods_used.method: Dice loss and Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE) loss combination
statistical_methods_used.purpose: To serve as the loss function for training the model, addressing class imbalance and optimizing segmentation accuracy.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance comparisons between the proposed EfficientUNet++ model and other models were based on direct metric values (Dice coefficient, IoU, Accuracy, Loss) without statistical significance testing.

PH2.notes: EfficientUNet++ achieved the best performance. The paper notes that SegNet performed relatively ineffective image segmentation. Sample imbalance in the PH2 dataset causes segmentation models to be severely biased.

ISIC-2018.notes: EfficientUNet++ achieved the highest scores in default performance measures. It showed similar Dice coefficient (96%) to other models but better IoU (94% vs 89%-93%) and loss value (11% vs 13%-11%). EfficientUNet++ has fewer trainable parameters than HRNeT.

Ablation Study (PH2 Validation Set).notes: Ablation study on different modules. EfficientNet + UNet++ yielded the best results.

Ablation Study (Activation Functions).notes: Ablation study on activation functions. ReLU provided better accuracy than other functions (Mish, Swish, GeLU, PReLU).

architecture_used.pretraining: Pretrained EfficientNet (B0) and Xception models were used as backbones for the encoder, initialized with weights from ImageNet.
architecture_used.input_size: 200x200 pixels (inferred from data augmentation parameters in Table 3: size(200,200))

Added residual units to deepen the network.
Redesigned skip connections to aggregate features of varying semantic scales.

conclusions: The proposed EfficientUNet++ model efficiently detects skin lesions by improving composite coefficients and structurally expanding the size of the convolution network.
The use of residual units deepens the network, which further improves segmentation performance.

EfficientUNet++ achieved superior performance compared to other models on the PH2 dataset, with a Dice coefficient of 93% (vs. 76%–91%), IoU of 96% (vs. 74%–95%), and lower loss value (30% vs. 44%–32%).
On the ISIC-2018 dataset, EfficientUNet++ obtained a similar Dice coefficient (96%) to other models but demonstrated better IoU (94% vs. 89%–93%) and loss value (11% vs. 13%–11%).
EfficientUNet++ exhibits higher robustness and better performance results on both datasets compared to the state-of-the-art HRNeT method.
EfficientUNet++ has fewer trainable parameters than HRNeT, making it more efficient in memory usage.
The proposed method improves accuracy and efficiency in medical image segmentation by making fuzzy boundaries clearer and enhancing focus on foreground pixels.
The lighter colors in lesion areas were not accurately segmented, indicating a limitation that requires further improvement.
important_remarks: The study acknowledges that sample imbalance in the PH2 dataset causes segmentation models to be severely biased, limiting prediction accuracy despite data augmentation.
The model has a specific limitation in accurately segmenting lesions with lighter colors, indicating a need for further improvement in handling low-contrast boundaries.
The paper does not report formal statistical hypothesis testing (e.g., p-values); performance comparisons are based solely on direct metric values (Dice, IoU) without significance tests.
The PH2 dataset is small (400 images total, 160 training) and sourced from a single institution (Pedro Hispano Hospital, Portugal), raising concerns about external validation and generalizability.
The ISIC-2018 dataset used for testing is significantly larger (2570 training, 200 testing) than PH2, potentially creating an imbalance in evaluation rigor between the two datasets.
The model's capacity to identify melanoma lesions is restricted by dataset limitations, and the method does not provide insight into melanoma prognosis.

Early stopping was used to mitigate overfitting (occurring after 33 epochs for PH2 and 27 epochs for ISIC-2018), but the final test results rely on weights from specific intermediate epochs rather than the final epoch weights.
The study focuses only on segmentation and does not address classification or diagnostic accuracy, which are critical for clinical deployment in melanoma detection.
